commands.review_summary:
The branch adds cooperative yielding and abort classification inside Ollama native NDJSON stream parsing, with dense-stream regression tests.

PR surface: Source +47, Tests +86. Total +133 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the scoped dense Ollama native-stream path: source inspection shows current main drains parsed NDJSON events without cooperative yields, and the PR adds focused dense-stream regression coverage. The live Windows local-daemon reproduction from the linked issue was not rerun here.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; a maintainer should decide whether to land this scoped P1 Ollama mitigation and keep the broader linked issue open.

target.title: fix(ollama): yield during dense stream processing
target.body:
## Summary

- ports the Ollama dense stream cooperative-yield fix from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 onto a current maintainer branch, preserving @udaymanish6 as commit author
- yields and checks aborts while draining dense native Ollama NDJSON chunks so one large read cannot monopolize the Gateway event loop
- preserves caller cancellation semantics during dense stream processing and adds regression coverage for both timer progress and abort classification

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.
Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 as the rebased maintainer branch.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: dense native Ollama stream chunks now cooperatively yield during parsing instead of draining an entire large read in one synchronous loop, while caller aborts still surface as `aborted` rather than provider `error`.

Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree, formatting/lint checks, branch autoreview, and AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts --reporter=dot
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 60m --ttl 180m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Evidence after fix: focused local wrapper passed `extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts` with 12/12 tests after the final rebase to `f4d461bbff6`; oxfmt, oxlint, and diff checks passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings with `overall: patch is correct (0.86)`; AWS Crabbox `check:changed` passed with provider `aws`, lease `cbx_0f647200aa61`, slug `coral-krill`, run `run_9c4b846bd7fc`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit 0, `leaseStopped=true`.

Observed result after fix: dense stream unit coverage confirms timer progress during parser drain, aborts during dense processing keep `reason` and assistant `stopReason` set to `aborted`, and changed extension type/lint/import-cycle guards passed remotely.

What was not tested: Windows local-daemon reproduction from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599 was not rerun in this PR. This is one mitigation in the broader event-loop starvation cluster, not the final issue closeout by itself.
target.author: vincentkoc
